title: Cotoneaster nummularius orbicularis
intro: Cotoneaster nummularius orbicularis, also known as the cushion cotoneaster, is a small shrub native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cotoneaster nummularius orbicularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cotoneaster nummularius orbicularis is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive problems and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cotoneaster nummularius orbicularis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Cotoneaster, which comprises approximately 70 species of shrubs and small trees. The family Rosaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as the rose family, which includes many edible fruits such as apples, pears, and plums. Cotoneaster nummularius orbicularis is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, where it thrives in rocky and alpine environments. The plant's specific classification is further supported by its unique characteristics and physical traits.
uses: Cotoneaster nummularius orbicularis, like other Cotoneaster species, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The berries and other parts of the plant are said to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. However, there is limited scientific evidence to support the effectiveness of Cotoneaster nummularius orbicularis for medicinal purposes. More research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and risks associated with using this plant for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Cotoneaster nummularius orbicularis contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. Flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Phenolic acids have been found to have antimicrobial and antitumor properties.
